what_it_does: A standalone preview card (@dsCard group="Brand") that depicts the staged-process flow pattern: a vertical ladder of white stage cards joined by solid-gold connectors with gap-pills (durations/actions) and a ⊕ join, paired with a right-hand gallery of escalating-fidelity render frames (grey massing → defined → detailed → hero) fed by image-slot custom elements.
observations: Links _card.css and loads image-slot.js for droppable render slots.
Left ladder uses .stage cards (2.5px gold border, r-20) and .conn connectors built from dot + seg lines + pill + ▼ arrow, plus a circular ⊕ join.
Gap-pills carry either an action ("Revision") or a muted duration ("1 – 2 Weeks") styled with --accent-bronze-2.
Right gallery has four frames at increasing heights (64/76/88/104px) with captions GREY MASSING / DEFINED / DETAILED / HERO RENDER.
image-slot elements point at ../assets/illustrations/staged/*.png with placeholder="Drop render".
Stages are hardcoded: Mobilisation (head), Stage 1/2/3, Marketing Package.
Meta footer summarizes the pattern's tokens (card / connector / gap / join) and lists related cards.
standouts: Render fidelity is encoded visually by escalating frame height plus caption, mirroring the project's process stages.
The lead text explicitly states vessels reuse the container + connector system and titles use --accent-gold-deep.
conventions: Follows the @dsCard preview convention (HTML comment metadata: group/name/subtitle/viewport).
Uses design tokens via var(--…) throughout (no raw hex except a shadow/text-shadow rgba).
Self-describing lead + meta footer naming related cards, matching the corpus self-description discipline.
notes: A brand preview card demoing the staged-deliverable ladder pattern with real escalating-fidelity render slots — the visual spec for how project stages are drawn.
questions: Do the referenced staged render PNGs actually exist at ../assets/illustrations/staged/, or are these still empty drop-slots?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the @dsCard subtitle (vertical stage ladder, gap-pills, ⊕ join, escalating-fidelity renders) is exactly what the markup builds.
apparent_themes: Token-driven visual language (gold connectors, bronze captions)
Reuse of one container/connector system across patterns
intention_signals: Codifies the staged-process / deliverable-flow as a reusable brand pattern so any process diagram in the product is drawn the same way, with the render-fidelity ramp as a built-in concept.
novelty: Encoding deliverable fidelity through frame height and tying gap-pills to either duration or action on a shared connector vessel.
contribution: Contributes the canonical visual treatment of a staged/iterative process flow (ladder + gold connectors + gap-pills + ⊕ join + escalating render gallery) that a fused system would keep as its process-diagram pattern.
summary_for_embedding: A ConceptV design-system brand preview card rendering the staged-process flow pattern as a vertical stage ladder with gold connectors, gap-pills, a ⊕ join, and an escalating-fidelity render gallery via image-slot elements.
intention_for_embedding: To codify the reusable visual pattern for staged/iterative deliverable processes in the ConceptV brand language.
